notes_md:
**chào đón** = to welcome, to greet warmly when someone/something arrives. It has a positive, open feeling.

It’s a verb. You use it when receiving a person, an event, or even a change in a positive way.  

It’s stronger and warmer than just **đón** (which is more neutral: pick up/receive).  
Opposite idea: **từ chối** (refuse) — that contrast helps you remember the “open arms” feeling.
exampleSentencesTranslated: My family always welcome you to visit anytime.
The new company warmly welcome new employees.
We should welcome creative ideas from everyone.

translation.en: We will welcome you when you arrive.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi sẽ ___ bạn khi bạn đến.

definitions.en: To greet or receive someone with pleasure.
definitions.es: Recibir o saludar a alguien con placer.
definitions.vi: Để chào hỏi hoặc tiếp đón ai đó với niềm vui.

example.en: We welcome you to the party.
example.es: Te damos la bienvenida a la fiesta.
example.vi: Chúng tôi chào đón bạn đến với bữa tiệc.

translation.en: We will welcome you when you arrive.

content.sentence: Chúng tôi sẽ ___ bạn khi bạn đến.

notes:
chào đón = to welcome, to greet warmly when someone/something arrives. It has a positive, open feeling.

It’s a verb. You use it when receiving a person, an event, or even a change in a positive way.  

It’s stronger and warmer than just đón (which is more neutral: pick up/receive).  
Opposite idea: từ chối (refuse) — that contrast helps you remember the “open arms” feeling.
exampleSentences: Gia đình anh luôn chào đón bạn đến chơi bất cứ lúc nào.
Công ty mới rất nhiệt tình chào đón các nhân viên mới.
Chúng ta nên chào đón những ý tưởng sáng tạo từ mọi người.
